data.title: Greeks.live: Trump's tariff policy may "trigger a nuclear explosion", the community is mainly bearish
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Greeks.live released a community briefing, pointing out that Trump’s tariff policy may "trigger a nuclear explosion". The community mainly holds a bearish view and expects prices to continue to fluctuate. Traders are closely watching Bitcoin in the price range of $85,000-87,000, regarding it as a key resistance area.

data.title: Binance Launches Second Round of Voting for Coin Listings
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to official news, Binance has launched the second round of voting for coin listings. Users can view the factors that determine the voting pool for coin listings in the first batch of voting for coin listings.

data.title: CME Reports Record High in Cryptocurrency Derivatives Trading Volume
data.description:
According to PANews, the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) Group has reported a record high in cryptocurrency derivatives trading volume for the first quarter of 2025. The average daily trading volume reached 198,000 contracts, with a nominal value of $11.3 billion. This marks a significant milestone for the exchange.

The trading volume for micro Ether futures reached 76,000 contracts, while micro Bitcoin futures saw a year-on-year increase of 113%, with an average daily trading volume of 77,000 contracts. The surge in trading activity is attributed to the lower thresholds and ease of risk management associated with micro contracts, which have contributed to the overall increase in trading activity.

data.title: U.S. ADP employment in March was 155,000, expected to be 115,000, and the previous value was 77,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The ADP employment in the United States in March was 155,000, the expected number was 115,000, and the previous value was 77,000. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's May Rate Decision Likely to Hold Steady
data.description: According to Odaily, the CME's "FedWatch" tool indicates an 83.5% probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain its current interest rates in May, ahead of the ADP data release. There is a 16.5% chance of a 25 basis point rate cut. Looking ahead to June, the probability of rates remaining unchanged is 25.1%, while the likelihood of a cumulative 25 basis point cut stands at 63.4%, and a 50 basis point cut is at 11.6%.

data.title: Ethereum Proposal Aims to Enhance Validator Exit Efficiency
data.description: According to Odaily, Ethereum researcher mikeneuder.eth has introduced EIP-7922, a proposal designed to improve the flexibility and efficiency of the validator exit queue while maintaining system security. Currently, Ethereum employs a fixed exit limit of approximately 16 exits per epoch, which ensures economic security by controlling the number of exits and preventing a decline in security. However, during periods of increased exit demand, validators face prolonged waiting times, leading to liquidity risks and higher capital costs. EIP-7922 suggests dynamically adjusting the exit volume based on recent actual exit activity, replacing the fixed limit. The proposal is currently in the draft stage.

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Continue Downward Trend
data.description: According to BlockBeats, market data indicates that U.S. stock index futures are experiencing continued declines. Nasdaq futures have fallen by 1%, while S&P 500 futures have decreased by 0.8%, and Dow Jones futures have dropped by 0.6%.

data.title: Trump announces "Liberation Day" schedule: plans to announce reciprocal tariffs at 4 a.m. Beijing time on April 3
Ethereum researchers propose EIP-7922 to improve the flexibility and efficiency of Ethereum exit queues
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Ethereum researcher mikeneuder.eth proposed EIP-7922, which aims to improve the flexibility and efficiency of the Ethereum validator exit queue while ensuring system security. It is reported that Ethereum currently uses a fixed exit limit (about 16 exits) for each epoch. Although it can ensure economic security (control the number of exits and prevent security from decreasing), when the demand for exits surges, validators need to wait for a long time, increasing liquidity risks and funding costs. EIP-7922 proposes to dynamically adjust the exit amount based on the actual exit situation in the near future, replacing the fixed limit. The proposal is currently in the draft stage.
